div.p: On the evening of November 7, 1951, President Truman delivered a radio and television report to the American people on international arms reduction, with particular reference to the United States United Kingdom-France declaration released in Paris earlier in the day. For text of the tripartite declaration, see telegram Delga 72, supra. For text of the President’s address, see Public Papers of the Presidents of the United States: Harry S. Truman, 1951, pages 623–627, or Department of State Bulletin, November 19, 1951, pages 799–803.
